title: malus rhotomagensis
intro: Malus rhotomagensis, also known as the Roraima apple or Roraima crab apple, is a species of apple tree native to the Guiana Shield region of South America. The medicinal properties of Malus rhotomagensis lie in its bark, which contains tannins and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections. Malus rhotomagensis is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and can also be used to make infusions or infuse in oil for topical applications.
